jobs.title: Systems Engineer (Linux & Automation)
jobs.shortDescription: Hands-on Systems Engineer to design, automate, optimize, and scale Linux production infrastructure with strong ownership and an automation-first mindset.
jobs.description: We’re looking for a hands-on Systems Engineer who understands Linux deeply and enjoys building infrastructure from the ground up. This is not a maintenance role. You will design, automate, optimize, and scale production systems. You should be comfortable debugging at the kernel level, writing clean automation scripts, and taking ownership of infrastructure decisions. If you enjoy solving hard system problems and hate repetitive manual work, you’ll fit right in.
responsibilities: Own and manage Linux-based production systems
Build and maintain infrastructure automation using Ansible, Puppet, or similar tools
Deploy and operate distributed systems, including Ceph or similar storage platforms
Write automation and tooling in Bash and Python
Debug performance bottlenecks across CPU, memory, I/O, and networking
Improve reliability, scalability, and system performance
Automate everything that repeats more than once
requirements: 3–8 years of relevant experience in systems engineering and Linux operations
Deep understanding of Linux processes, memory, and filesystems
Hands-on experience with systemd and service management
Comfortable using tools such as strace, tcpdump, iostat, vmstat, perf, and lsof
Experience with Ansible, Puppet, or similar infrastructure automation tools
Comfortable writing production-grade Bash and Python scripts
Belief in and practical adoption of Infrastructure-as-Code
Ability to build infrastructure from scratch and take ownership in ambiguous environments
preferred: Experience with distributed storage systems such as Ceph or ZFS
Exposure to high-performance or GPU-based environments
